subject: [PATCH v2 2/4] memcg: uint16_t for nr_bytes in obj_stock_pcp

reviews.author: Qi Zheng
reviews.summary: Reviewer Qi Zheng suggested changing the refill condition in __refill_obj_stock() to prevent refilling more than U16_MAX bytes, which would cause nr_bytes to overflow.
reviews.raw_body:
^

should also be changed to: don't refill > U16_MAX ?

Otherwise:

Acked-by: Qi Zheng <qi.zheng@linux.dev>

Thanks!

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch needs further revision and promised to either fix the issue in a new version or request Andrew to make an in-place change.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks. If I send a new version, I will fix this otherwise I will ask Andrew to
fix this inplace.

2026-05-22.patch_summary: This patch updates the 'nr_bytes' field in the 'struct obj_stock_pcp' from an unsigned int to a uint16_t to reduce memory usage on per-CPU caches. The change is necessary because the current implementation can overflow on larger page sizes, such as 256KB pages. To prevent this, the patch accumulates new totals in an unsigned int local variable and flushes whenever the accumulator would hit U16_MAX. Additionally, it uses atomic_add to push any sub-page remainder above U16_MAX into objcg->nr_charged_bytes for configurations with PAGE_SHIFT > 16.
